start.line: More tea, Mr. Grey? We have simply ages. Well. Until four.

options.text: Your device has a flaw. They always do.
You'll never get away with this.
No more tea for me, thank you.

why.line: The Royal Garden Party, 1961. My petit-fours — CUT from the programme. For SCONES, Mr. Grey. When the world stops at 4:01, no one will ever be served before me again.

options.text: (Do not, under any circumstances, mention who you work for.)

cakes.line: JUST CAKES?! Mr. Fondant, show him the— no. No. I am calm. I am glazed and calm.

flaw.line: Flaw! The escapement is Swiss, the casing is Swiss, the WARRANTY is Swiss. Nothing short of a sub-atomic jiffy of brine could confuse it, and I hardly keep anchovies, Mr. Grey.

options.text: No olives at YOUR garden party, then.

olives.line: Olives are a SAVOURY, Mr. Grey. This is a TEA. Do try to be civilised.

never.line: Get away? Dear boy, I'm not going anywhere. That is rather the POINT of stopping the clocks.
